topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment felt thick, suffocating. I watched him across the dinner table, a small, almost imperceptible smile playing on his lips as he effortlessly navigated a complex phrase in my mother tongue. It wasn't just casual conversation; it was fluency. A sudden, hot prickle started at my collarbone and spread, tightening my chest until I had to consciously draw a slow, shallow breath. I picked up my fork, the clink against the porcelain sounding unnaturally loud. "That was... impressive," I managed, the words tasting like ash. I focused intently on arranging the food on my plate, making sure every placement was precise, a silent declaration that I was perfectly content with my current, unenhanced state. Later, when he was engrossed in a book, I stood up abruptly, needing air. "I'm going for a walk," I announced, my voice clipped and too bright. I didn't wait for a reply, just walked out, the pavement burning beneath my feet as I channeled the sudden, sharp feeling inside me into a brisk, angry stride.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked inside his old textbook—a highlighted passage in my grandmother's dialect. My jaw locked so tight I thought my molars might crack. A slow, cold burn began in my stomach, a deep, heavy pressure that made my hands tremble slightly as I gripped the paper. He hadn't told me. He had been absorbing it, quietly, meticulously. The realization wasn't just about the language; it was about the subtle shift in our dynamic, the unspoken knowledge he was accumulating. I retreated to the bedroom, pacing the length of the rug. Every movement was jerky, fueled by a simmering resentment. When he finally looked up, his expression was one of gentle confusion, which only sharpened the unpleasant sensation within me. I met his gaze, my eyes cold and flat, and offered a response that was drier than sandpaper. "It's… interesting," I said, the inflection carefully neutral, a performance designed to mask the churning dissatisfaction boiling beneath my skin.

[story 3]

The sound of his voice, rolling and melodic in my native tongue, hit me like a physical blow. It was flawless, nuanced, something I had only ever heard spoken by my own mother. A strange, tight knot formed just beneath my sternum, constricting my ability to relax. I kept my expression carefully blank, forcing a slow, deliberate sip of my drink, trying to keep my breathing even despite the internal turbulence. He was laughing, completely absorbed in a conversation, and the ease with which he wielded the language felt like a deliberate slight. I watched him, cataloging every inflection, every perfectly placed word, as if dissecting an enemy. When the conversation finally ended, I pushed my chair back with unnecessary force, the scrape of the wood against the floor echoing the sudden, sharp dismissal I felt toward the entire situation. "I need some space," I stated, my tone laced with an unfamiliar, brittle sharpness, determined to make him feel the weight of my displeasure.
